steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The student wants to describe the researchers' methodology — not the goal, not the results, but what they actually did. A methodology statement covers the experimental setup: what was tested, under what conditions, with what controls.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'stating the research goal': Describes what the researchers hoped to find, not what they did. ✗
  • Choice B — 'stating a general result': Describes an outcome, not a procedure. ✗
  • Choice C — 'describing the test conditions': Identifies that 125 ingested seeds and 125 non-ingested control seeds were used — exactly the experimental setup. ✓
  • Choice D — 'stating one specific result number': Gives a single data point from the findings, not the methodology. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
